title: Using Monday.com Offline in Desktop App
excerpt:
Hi team, 
Would love to have an offline version of Monday.com for those of us who spend a lot of times on the planes and in super remote places :). Ideally, the offline version would sync with the online version once the…

posts.author: ElenaK (Elena Korolkova (Kick Consulting))
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi team,

Would love to have an offline version of Monday.com for those of us who spend a lot of times on the planes and in super remote places :). Ideally, the offline version would sync with the online version once the user is back online. Thank you! Love your work.

posts.author: Galit-monday.com (Galit)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks for sharing this feedback and thank you for your compliment! We’ve had this request in the past but it’s not so in demand. We are documenting and tracking this to see if this is possible and how to implement this in the future. I’ll share this with our product team so they can use this as reference!

posts.author: Eltjo (Eltjo Smit)
posts.cookedHtml: To be honest I think this would be really great but for me it is indeed a low prio feature.

posts.author: Bastian (Bastian Fricke)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1 for Offline Mode

We have a lot of technicians who work with monday and in often at building lots the have no internet connection.
I will love it to use the offline mode during travelling by plane also

posts.author: kcarter (Kris Carter)
posts.cookedHtml:
Offline is a critical feature for anyone who

  1. Spends time on planes
  2. Spends time in developing countries

Moreover, ALL your competitors function offline through their apps. The web version doesn’t need to work offline, but if your mobile and desktop apps don’t work offline, that minimizes one of the main reasons for even having an app.

I paid lots of $$ to use Monday.com because I love it so much, but I’m currently having to switch everything over to my FREE Trello account just so I can get work done on planes and in Africa. I’m trying to run an international organization and this lack of functionality (that even free apps already have) is making Monday.com useless to me.

posts.author: abotero (Antonia)
posts.cookedHtml:
This would definitely be useful for my team as we travel all over the US and often find ourselves without internet (trains, planes). This is especially difficult for when we have meetings and need to record all minutes that require action. Currently, we make lists on excel and then batch copy to the corresponding board whenever we get some access. This causes a delay between the time when we have the meeting and when our team members not present get the notification that action is required.
More complex usability in offline mode would be the cherry on top, but this basic use is really impacting our team.
Aside from this, we are loving Monday!!! We even fully migrated to it to get past some of the awkward yoga poses our previous software was forcing us into

posts.author: cjbasie (Vasiliy Filatov)
posts.cookedHtml:
It would be awesome to have this feature!
all my friends is crazy about Monday when see how I use it and how it helps me. But you right. Planes, trains, travel thoughts push me to use notepad or other soft to fix my thougts on paper and then (when the Internet connection appear), manage it in Monday

posts.author: aperegrine (Adam Peregrine)
posts.cookedHtml: This would be great for me too, i sell headstones for a living and spend a lot of time in churches and cemeteries, neither of which are always within reach of internet, i often need to get onto monday.com to see the customer info and find myself unable to.

posts.author: Jack-JTsCloud (Jack Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: Had a request for this today also, would be a great feature.

posts.author: editarr (Ed.Arr)
posts.cookedHtml: For anyone using the app on their phones an offline feature is vital. I don’t always have reception and I still need to access Monday and my tasks. I am thinking of switching to another app because I really need this feature.

posts.author: Torstein (Torstein Fosse)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1 request for this feature

It is very necessary for who of us how are travelling a lot on plains

posts.author: chen (C)
posts.cookedHtml: Please implement this feature. I have 4-6 flights a month and I find that a flight without any access to wifi is the best time to review tasks, clean up my backlog and plan future tasks. It would be super helpful to be able to use Monday on flights.

posts.author: AdriannaAmaseu (Adrianna)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Monday team!
Can you provide an estimate when the offline version is intended to be realeased?
Seems like demand is quite high. My team keeps asking me.

posts.author: max.haines-stiles (Max Haines-Stiles)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1

Agreed, offline mode in the Monday.com desktop app would be very useful for anyone who travels extensively.

posts.author: bowersmortgage (Eric A. Bowers)
posts.cookedHtml: Agree. This is necessary. I found myself at an open house on Saturday, unable to add a potential client to my database as spotty cell coverage and no wifi. I’m shopping for competitors.

posts.author: vancestone (Vance)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Monday. What is the update on this feature? Our sales team travels a and right now we cant use monday on flights or offsite. You say no demand but surely we are not the only people traveling and working!

posts.author: fREsUB (Free Sub)
posts.cookedHtml: Offline mode is part of the future ecosystem which is happening now!

posts.author: jjaylewin (Jake Jay-Lewin)
posts.cookedHtml: I often need to work offline, on planes or in spotty signal areas; not being able to track my tasks or plan my projects offline is pretty debilitating. I don’t need full functionality, but I do need to be able to work on individual boards and pulse’s

posts.author: bradzach4 (Brad Zacharias)
posts.cookedHtml: Offline would be extremely useful as my team is frequently in locations without internet access.

posts.author: Arnaud (Arnaud Prigent)
posts.cookedHtml: Offline would be extremely useful now that my team is confined in locations with limited internet access

posts.author: Paulk (Paul Konijnendijk)
posts.cookedHtml:
Really need Monday offline, we use it in remote areas as well.
Is it planned? When can we expect it?

posts.author: Paulk (Paul Konijnendijk)
posts.cookedHtml: When is the ecosystem happening?

posts.author: DerekErb (Derek Erb)
posts.cookedHtml:
Is there a place to vote for this feature to show cumulated interest?

I travel often and need to be able to modify my projects and time-tracking and prepare updates for clients and suppliers while offline.

As mentioned previously this can be a “deal breaker” for some/many of us as many of Monday’s comparable competitors offer this functionality.

How do we vote for, and emphasise the need for, this feature?

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @DerekErb, you have a Vote Button on top of each thread.

posts.author: DerekErb (Derek Erb)
posts.cookedHtml: Thank you for the Vote Button information.

posts.author: TripFoster (Trip Foster)
posts.cookedHtml: this feature is key-- going to be hard to justify Pro license without it. You should provide a roadmap on this feature. Other Apps like Todoist have this feature built in

posts.author: KevinT (Kevin)
posts.cookedHtml: Same here - we have a Pro licence but without this feature we have to cancel it.

posts.author: brentpacelive (Brent Pace)
posts.cookedHtml:
I am absolutely going to add that our team are needing this more and more. We run a touring entertainment company and are on tour busses, planes and going through areas without service. We always make our Google Docs available offline as it’s the only way we can operate. I love the product, but we are starting to have to revert to other systems as we just don’t have the ability to use this all the time as much as we would like to.
Please consider this feature.

posts.author: lfmt (Laureen)
posts.cookedHtml: I am also voting for this feature. I love Monday! (the app, not the actual day hehe) but the inability to access this while offline is hindering me to use it fully as I’m scared that my changes will be lost while online. Or my new changes are not reflected due to internet connection. I hope the development team can consider this please.

posts.author: zaneta (Zaneta Grant)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone! Thrilled to announce that we now have offline mode for our mobile apps
You can find out more here: https://support.monday.com/hc/en-us/articles/360017712819-Mobile-App-Offline-Mode

Our team is constantly looking for ways to improve the functionality of our mobile apps, so stay tuned for more improvements. Thanks for your feedback and for you patience!

posts.author: system (system)
posts.cookedHtml: This topic was automatically closed 7 days after the last reply. New replies are no longer allowed.

posts.author: jeannel (jeannel)
posts.cookedHtml: We totally need this for desktop- especially when dealing with much more information; when working on planes or when there is no connectivity. Imagine having a wifi situation and you can’t present your dashboard as there is no offline mode.

posts.author: MartinPtarmingan (Martin  Skinner)
posts.cookedHtml:
Is there any update to this?

We have recently started using Monday a lot but as an integration company we are regally on building sites or cruise ships that have no wifi or reception to tether a laptop to your phone.

To me this would be a priority for development

posts.author: ubaldot (Ubaldo)
posts.cookedHtml: Great job for the mobile app, but what is the current status for the desktop?

posts.author: Elf (Elfo)
posts.cookedHtml: Thinking about giving monday a chance to replace some other tools. BUT… Offline usability is essential for us on Tablet and Desktop. Is this implemented already?

posts.author: CMG (Chris Greenwood)
posts.cookedHtml:
Like the other contributors, the ability to have offline functionality for the desktop app is crucial. My Team performs ship inspections where there is often no internet - we cannot issue our reports via the tool without this.
This worked well in my previous company - any updates made were synced when internet access was restored. Please prioritize this function.
